decision: Frontend framework: Blazor Server
rationale: Full .NET stack, SSR suits compliance UIs, matches ASP.NET Core lock

decision: Auth provider: Firebase Auth
rationale: Native Firestore integration, OIDC/JWT, MFA built-in

decision: Cloud hosting: Azure (SA North) + Firestore via Google SDK
rationale: SA data residency for POPIA, cross-cloud supported pattern

decision: Payroll cadence: Monthly AND Weekly
rationale: Many SA SMEs in retail/manufacturing pay weekly. Overrides ASSUME-006.

decision: Leave types in v1: Annual + Sick + Family + Maternity + Parental
rationale: BCEA mandates all five types

decision: PDF generation: QuestPDF
rationale: Open source, .NET native, suitable for payslips and evidence packs

decision: Domain event bus: MediatR (in-process)
rationale: Modular monolith pattern, synchronous within module, async for cross-module

decision: SARS filing channel: CSV export for e@syFile import (not direct API)
rationale: Standard channel for SMEs, avoids SARS API complexity

decision: HTML mockups in docs/design/mockups/ replace Google Stitch as UI source of truth
rationale: Mockups already built (8 screens + shared.css) implementing the ZenoHR design system. Stitch prompts served their purpose for design ideation. Building directly from HTML mockups gives pixel-level fidelity guidance for Blazor components.

decision: ZenoHR Context MCP server built with FastMCP 3.0 (Python via uv) — not TypeScript
rationale: uv enables Python without a pre-installed Python runtime. FastMCP 3.0 provides cleaner tool definitions. stdio transport works natively with Claude Code MCP integration.

decision: Agent git autonomy: stage-only (git add + summary), no auto-commit
rationale: User retains commit control. Agents stage specific files and show summary. User manually runs git commit after reviewing staged changes.

decision: Firebase project not yet created — add to Phase 0 as TASK-034
rationale: Firebase project needed before Firestore emulator can be configured. Must be created before TASK-020 (Firestore connection). Added TASK-034, TASK-035, TASK-036 to Phase 0.

decision: 7-role model replaced with 5-tier role architecture (SaasAdmin, Director, HRManager, Manager, Employee)
rationale: Simpler, more practical for SA SMEs. Director and HRManager have identical full access. Manager roles are dynamic/department-scoped. Eliminates specialized PayrollOfficer/ComplianceOfficer/Auditor roles — Director/HRManager handle those functions. SecurityAdmin replaced by SaasAdmin platform role.

decision: Users see ONLY their role's screens — absent entirely from nav, not greyed/disabled
rationale: Cleaner UX. Each role experiences the app purely from their POV. No confusion about disabled features. Confirmed by user.

decision: Dynamic RBAC: Director/HRManager create custom Manager role variants per department via Role Management UI
rationale: Supports SA SME diversity — Finance Manager, Warehouse Manager, etc. each scoped to own department. Permission tokens constrained to 6 allowed Manager-level tokens. Payroll/compliance/audit cannot be granted to any Manager.

decision: Employees have NO timesheet access — HR/supervisors enter timesheets only
rationale: Confirmed by user. Simplifies employee self-service scope. HR enters hours based on attendance records/clock system.

decision: Employment identity and system access role are separate concerns. Every Director/HRManager/Manager/Employee has an employees document (HR/payroll identity). system_role is access-only overlay. SaasAdmin has no employee record.
rationale: Directors, HRManagers, and Managers are employed by the tenant company — they receive payslips, accrue leave, are subject to PAYE/UIF/BCEA exactly like any other employee. Confirmed by user. SaasAdmin is platform operator only.
impacts: employees collection — every tenant user has a document

decision: Multiple active user_role_assignments per user supported. Effective role = highest privilege. Multi-dept managers get combined team view. Dual-role persons have one payroll record tied to primary department.
rationale: SA SMEs need flexibility: one person may manage two departments simultaneously, or hold a management role in one department while being an operative in another. One payroll record per person per period (confirmed by user). Combined team view chosen over department switcher (confirmed by user).
impacts: API team queries — WHERE department_id IN [...]

title: Director/HRManager leave is self-approved
decision: Director and HRManager leave requests are automatically self-approved — no human approver is required or assigned. The AuditEvent records actor == requester with a `SELF_APPROVED_LEAVE` purpose code. The leave ledger is updated immediately on submission.
rationale: There is no role above Director/HRManager within a tenant. Requiring external approval would block the feature entirely. Self-approval is auditable and consistent with how payroll co-approval exception is handled for the same roles (REQ-SEC-003).

title: Executive system department auto-created at tenant onboarding
decision: A system department named "Executive" (is_system_dept: true) is automatically created for every new tenant during SaasAdmin provisioning. All Director and HRManager employee records have department_id pointing to this department. The Executive department cannot be deactivated, renamed, or deleted by tenant users.
rationale: All employee documents require a non-null department_id for payroll cost-centre allocation and leave routing. Directors/HRManagers are employees (REQ-HR-004) but do not belong to operational departments. A system-managed Executive department resolves the referential integrity requirement without exposing the implementation detail to tenant users.

title: Manager's own employee record included in team view
decision: When a Manager queries their team (employees, leave, timesheets), their own employee record is included in the result set alongside their direct reports. The Firestore query uses WHERE department_id IN [...managed_depts] with no exclusion for the requesting user's own employee_id.
rationale: Managers are employees of their own department. Excluding themselves from team views would create confusing inconsistency — their name would be absent from headcount reports and team leave calendars they manage. Inclusion is also required for self-access guarantee enforcement: the Manager must be able to see their own data through the team interface as well as the self-service interface.

title: Director/HRManager dashboard shows personal employee widgets
decision: The Director and HRManager dashboard includes personal employee widgets (own leave balance, own next payslip date) in addition to their full tenant-wide KPI widgets. These personal widgets are sourced from their own employees document and payroll_runs results, accessed via the self-access guarantee path (firebase_uid == requesting_uid).
rationale: Director/HRManager are employees subject to BCEA and SARS obligations. Omitting personal widgets would force them to navigate to a separate employee self-service screen for information that should be at a glance. The self-access data fetch adds one lightweight Firestore read — no architectural overhead.

title: Pre-aggregated analytics_snapshots collection for company analytics
decision: Use a dedicated analytics_snapshots Firestore collection materialized nightly by AnalyticsSnapshotService (BackgroundService), with event-triggered recomputation on PayrollRunFinalized and ComplianceChecked MediatR events. UI reads 1–12 snapshot documents per chart instead of N employee documents.
rationale: Firestore cannot efficiently run GROUP BY aggregations across hundreds of employees, payroll_results, or leave_balances documents per page load. Pre-aggregation at write time keeps read latency predictable regardless of tenant size (10–500 employees).

title: Personal analytics reads directly from own collections (no snapshot)
decision: The /my-analytics page reads own payroll_results and leave_balances directly via the self-access guarantee path (firebase_uid == requesting_uid) without requiring analytics_snapshots pre-aggregation.
rationale: An employee's own payslip and leave data is at most 12–24 documents per tax year (one per pay period). Direct Firestore reads are fast enough at personal scope. Adding snapshot complexity for personal data would be premature over-engineering with no performance benefit.

title: Security Operations Collections — Platform-Level, Admin SDK Only
decision: security_incidents, vulnerability_findings, and auth_anomaly_events are platform-level (cross-tenant) Firestore collections with Firestore security rules set to `allow read, write: if false`. All access is via Firebase Admin SDK from ZenoHR backend services only. SaasAdmin reads through the API — never directly via client SDK.
rationale: Security operations data must be invisible to all tenant users (cannot list incidents affecting other tenants, cannot read vulnerability scan results). Admin SDK bypasses Firestore rules, so the deny-all rules act as a safety net against misconfiguration. This matches the pattern used for audit_events immutability enforcement.

title: My Profile merged screen (/profile) — HR record + Account Settings in one route
decision: The /profile route renders a merged screen with: (1) employee HR record — read-only for the employee themselves, editable by HR Manager/Director; (2) Account Settings — always editable by the authenticated user (photo, display name, theme, notifications, MFA). No separate "My Account" tab on Settings screen.
rationale: Single merged screen eliminates nav confusion. Employee does not need two places to manage their identity. Director/HRManager manage employee records from the same /employees/{id} route. Settings screen remains admin-only (Users, Departments, Company, Security, Archival).

title: AuditEvent canonical JSON excludes EventHash; uses fixed field order
decision: AuditEvent.ToCanonicalJson() serialises 11 fields in fixed declaration order using System.Text.Json anonymous-type serialisation. EventHash is intentionally excluded (it is the output of hashing, not an input). genesis events use empty string "" for previous_event_hash in canonical JSON (not null) for consistent hashing. SHA-256 computed via SHA256.HashData + Convert.ToHexStringLower.
rationale: Fixed field order guarantees deterministic hashing across .NET versions and server restarts. Excluding EventHash from canonical JSON allows the same method to be used both for initial hash computation and for verification. Empty-string sentinel for genesis previous_event_hash avoids null serialisation variance.

title: CQRS contracts (ICommand/IQuery/IHandler) placed in ZenoHR.Domain shared kernel
decision: ICommand, ICommand<TResult>, IQuery<TResult>, ITenantScoped, ICommandHandler<TCommand>, ICommandHandler<TCommand,TResult>, IQueryHandler<TQuery,TResult> all live in ZenoHR.Domain/Contracts/ namespace. They extend MediatR IRequest and IRequestHandler directly.
rationale: Domain is the shared kernel that all modules depend on. Placing CQRS contracts in Domain means every module gets them without circular references. Modules only need to reference ZenoHR.Domain to implement the full CQRS pattern.

title: BaseFirestoreRepository uses abstract NotFoundErrorCode property
decision: Each derived Firestore repository declares a protected abstract ZenoHrErrorCode NotFoundErrorCode property. GetByIdAsync uses it to return the module-specific error code (e.g., EmployeeNotFound, PayrollRunNotFound).
rationale: Avoids stringly-typed errors and preserves the error taxonomy defined in ZenoHrErrorCode, while keeping the base class generic and reusable.

title: JwtBearer is NOT in ASP.NET Core 10 shared framework — explicit NuGet reference required
decision: Added Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer 10.0.0 as an explicit PackageReference in ZenoHR.Api.csproj.
rationale: Confirmed via inspection of dotnet shared folder: JwtBearer.dll is absent from Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 10.0.3. Must be referenced as a standalone NuGet package even in Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web projects.

title: GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline Structure (5 jobs)
decision: 5-job CI pipeline: (1) build gate — TreatWarningsAsErrors enforced; (2) unit-tests — solution-level with Integration filter excluded, XPlat Code Coverage; (3) integration-tests — Firebase emulator via JDK 21 + firebase-tools CLI, nc -z health check; (4) dependency-scan — dotnet list package --vulnerable --include-transitive, fails on CVE; (5) codeql — parallel SAST with security-extended queries. All jobs use global.json for SDK version and NuGet package cache. Emulator binaries cached separately.
rationale: Standard GitHub Actions pattern: build gate prevents wasted compute. Unit and integration tests run in parallel after build. Dependency scan is independent (no build artifact needed). CodeQL runs independently (observes its own build). Emulator cache (JARs ~100MB) avoids repeated Firebase CLI downloads. global-json-file ensures exact SDK version match with developer machines.

title: Coverage Enforcement: coverlet 90% line gate + ReportGenerator 85% branch gate
decision: Two-gate approach: (1) coverlet.collector via coverage.runsettings enforces >=90% line on ZenoHR.Domain+ZenoHR.Module.* — dotnet test fails on threshold violation. (2) ReportGenerator JsonSummary parsed by Python/jq to enforce >=85% branch on same assemblies. Threshold applies to ThresholdStat=total (aggregate across all included assemblies). Empty module AssemblyMarker classes contribute 0 coverable lines and do not affect the gate.
rationale: coverlet.collector natively supports line threshold enforcement (fail-fast during test run). Branch threshold needs ReportGenerator because coverlet data collector's branch enforcement can be unreliable in multi-project solutions. JsonSummary format is machine-readable and stable. Two separate mechanisms avoid a single point of failure and keep gate intent explicit in source control (coverage.runsettings).

title: MSFTC calculated outside PAYE engine, applied by orchestrator
decision: MsfTcCalculationEngine is a separate pure static class. PayrollOrchestrationService applies MSFTC after calling PayeCalculationEngine: net_paye = max(0, calculated_paye - monthly_msftc). PAYE engine remains unaware of medical scheme dependants.
rationale: Keeps PAYE engine focused on a single responsibility (bracket/rebate calculation). MSFTC is an employee-level credit that varies by individual, not a tax table rule. Separation allows MSFTC to be zero (non-members) without any special PAYE logic. Mirrors how SARS treats them conceptually — PAYE is the gross calculation; MSFTC is a credit applied against it.

title: Retention clock starts from termination_date not record creation
decision: 5-year data retention period is calculated from employee termination_date. Records for active employees are retained indefinitely. Records for terminated employees are archived (read-only, not deleted) 5 years after termination_date. Purge eligibility at 7 years requires formal legal approval.
rationale: SARS Tax Administration Act s29 requires records for 5 years from date the return is submitted or the last entry was made. BCEA s31 requires 3 years. Best practice and POPIA minimisation principle: retain for 5 years from employee exit, then archive; purge at 7 years only with legal sign-off. Using termination_date ensures a former employee's records are accessible during the 5-year window SARS can raise assessments against the employer.

title: ITREG is a data-export workflow, not a direct SARS API call
decision: Employee income tax registration (ITREG) is implemented as a data-export + guided workflow. ZenoHR exports employee details in SARS-required format; HR Manager submits via SARS eFiling manually. Direct SARS ITREG API is deferred to Phase 6 (requires ISV accreditation).
rationale: Direct SARS API access requires IBIR-006 ISV accreditation, which is a Phase 6 milestone. The export approach delivers immediate value (removes the manual data-gathering burden) without blocking launch on accreditation. CTL-SARS-006 blocking already prevents payroll from running without a valid tax reference, so the workflow is complete enough for compliance.

title: Annual tax table activation requires Compliance Officer approval gate
decision: New SARS statutory tables (PAYE brackets, UIF ceiling, ETI bands, MSFTC credits) are imported as draft StatutoryRuleSet documents. An automated regression pack runs against boundary scenarios. Activation requires Compliance Officer explicit approval. A TaxTableVersionActivated domain event is emitted on activation, creating an AuditEvent.
rationale: Tax tables directly affect every payroll calculation. An incorrect activation would produce wrong PAYE for all employees. The human approval gate (Compliance Officer review of regression results) provides a critical safety check before any production payroll runs against the new tables. The AuditEvent ensures traceability of who activated which version and when.

title: Security vulnerability register + documentation version control system
decision: Introduced: (1) docs/security/vulnerability-register.md — living 28-finding register (6 Sev-1, 12 Sev-2, 8 Sev-3, 2 Sev-4) from comprehensive OWASP/POPIA/BCEA audit. (2) docs/security/popia-control-status.md — 15-control POPIA implementation tracker (13% done). (3) Five new MCP tools: get_vulnerability_register(), get_popia_status(), get_doc_staleness(), bump_doc_version(), generate_traceability_index(). (4) Fixed MOCKUP_MAP (missing 12-16) and PRD_MAP (missing 16-17) in MCP server. (5) .github/workflows/doc-update-gate.yml — CI gate: traceability check, PRD staleness (45-day limit), security doc presence, traceability index artifact. (6) scripts/doc-audit.ps1 — local PowerShell health report. (7) CLAUDE.md updated with Security Gap Tracking and Documentation Governance sections.
rationale: Audit revealed: 6 Sev-1 production blockers (no security headers, no CORS, no MFA on privileged ops, no incident response, no breach register, no break-glass process); POPIA readiness only 13% (8 of 15 controls not started — criminal liability risk); PRD docs frozen since Feb 18 while code evolved; MCP server missing mockups 12-16 and PRDs 16-17; no CI enforcement preventing doc/code drift. All these gaps must be tracked and closed before v1.0 release.
